name: Jim Hall
abstract: James Stanley Hall (December 4, 1930 – December 10, 2013) was an American jazz guitarist, composer and arranger.

interviews.title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: 20th Century Innovations: Early Experiments with Jazz
20th Century Innovations: Recent Developments in Jazz

Newport Jazz Festival: Salute to Tal Farlow
Kool Jazz Festival: A Tribute to Wes Montgomery
JVC Jazz Festival: Ron Carter and Friends
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript mentions Jim Hall in the context of a trio (Jim Hall, Jimmy Giuffre, and Bob Brookmeyer) working opposite Marian McPartland's trio at The Composer club. There is no statement from Marian McPartland indicating a direct personal or professional relationship with Jim Hall himself, only t
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: I'd done a duo record with Jim Hall at the Playboy Club... I've known Jim almost forty years... my appearances with Jim are documented, and there are some lovely records that came out of my association with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: We went on tour in 1967. ... The tour was Thelonious and Sarah Vaughan, Barney Kessel, Jim Hall. I was part of the guitar trio – Barney Kessel, Jim Hall, and myself
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: I asked Jim if [he] would like to work with me. He said yeah, so we worked together. ... We had a very good quartet together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Collette
about_quotes.evidence: And then what he’d finally do... So we all got ideas that it was a better sounding band with him playing cello. It really was because Jim Hall was there with the chords... We all felt the same way... we said well this band really sounds good this way.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Collette interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: I had heard Jim Hall... And I just liked what he did. Unfortunately for me I got Jim Hall at a bad time when he was not physically well so he didn't play as well on that date as he played on some other dates that I had heard before and after.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
